description: Inline assembly that writes to memory without preserving the Solidity free-memory pointer (at `0x40`) can corrupt ABI encoding for later calls or returns. Downstream decodes may misread buffers, leading to unexpected reverts or data leaks.
name: Corrupt Free Memory Pointer in Assembly
remediation: - Save and restore the free-memory pointer when using custom memory writes. - Prefer Solidity primitives unless assembly is necessary; encapsulate unsafe sections. - Use formalized patterns for encoding/decoding (e.g., `abi.encode`) to avoid manual pointer math.
